content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                                  EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

AIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento 95814
Ph 322-5660


                                                                September 8, 1975


Mr. Fred P. Cooper
Supervisor Third District
1221 Oak Street, Suite 536
Oakland, California 94612

                                                    Re: Request for Opinion
                                                        No. 75-165

Dear Mr. Cooper:

     Thank you for your letter of August 19, 1975, re-
questing an opinion with respect to your responsibilities
as a lobbyist under the Political Reform Act. Since
your request raises no substantial question involving
interpretation of the Political Reform Act, your request
for a formal opinion is denied.

     Chapter 6 of the Political Reform Act makes it clear
that as a registered lobbyist you are prohibited from
contributing to any elective state official or candidate
and from arranging for or soliciting contributions to
such candidates. Moreover, your position as a supervisor
does not exempt you from these restrictions.

     If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact Ted Prim, a member of the Commission
staff.

                                                    Sincerely,

                                                    [signature]
                                                    Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                                    Chairman, for the
                                                    Commission

DHL:gp

BOARD OF SUPERVISORS

August 19, 1975

Fair Political Practices Commission
1015 "L" Street
Sacramento, CA

Gentlemen:

Over the past fifteen years as a private lawyer, I have been active in
representing clients before the State Public Utilities Commission with
regard to investigations and proposed changes in the regulations applying to public utilities by the PUC.

During the same period I have been active in the campaigns of a number
of candidates in Alameda County, including candidates for state office
and the State Legislature, and have on occasion solicited campaign
contributions for these candidates.

As I understand the present law, if I continue in the first activity I
will be required to register as a lobbyist before the PUC, and such
registration will preclude me from contributing to candidates for state
office and for soliciting campaign contributions on their behalf.

I have the following questions:

1. Does registration as a lobbyist with the State Public Utilities
Commission prohibit my contributing to the candidates of persons running for all state offices, including the legislature, or only from
contributing to the campaign of the Governor, who appoints the members
of the State PUC?

2. Does my registration as a lobbyist before the State Public Utilities
Commission prohibit my arranging or soliciting contributions for candidates for all state offices or only for the Governor, who appoints the
members of the PUC?

3. Since I also lobby the legislature and the Governor as a County Supervisor, which activities are exempt under the law, would I be able to continue making contributions and soliciting contributions on the ground that

1221 OAK STREET • SUITE 530 • OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94607 • 273-0647

August 19, 1975
Page 2

I am an exempt lobbyist for the County of Alameda, even though I might
also be a registered lobbyist before the FCC.

I would appreciate your response to these questions at your convenience.

Yours truly,

[signature]
Fred P. Cooper

FFC.g
